sanskrit:
शरीरं यद् अवाप्नोति यच् चाप्य् उत्क्रामतीश्वरः।
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धान् इवाशयात्॥
transliteration: śarīraṃ yad avāpnoti yac cāpy utkrāmatīśvaraḥ | gṛhītvaitāni saṃyāti vāyurgandhān ivāśayāt ||
literal_meaning: The lord of the body (soul) takes with him the subtle impressions when he leaves one body and enters another, just as the wind carries fragrances from their sources.
contextual_meaning: Our psychological imprints—habits, memories, values—are the only luggage we carry across life transitions; they determine the quality of our next experience.
psychological_state: Post-decision clarity: Arjuna realizes that every choice seeds future mind-states, so he feels both empowered and accountable.
trigger_scenario: A professional on the verge of retirement wonders, 'What part of “me” will show up in the next chapter—my cynicism or my curiosity?'
anchor_text: Your habits today shape tomorrow’s world.
